title: rubus fruticosus subsp. macrophyllus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. macrophyllus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or giant Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant that is native to the Himalayan region. The leaves and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. macrophyllus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies and supplements.
